channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: US Winter 2025-2026: Record Warmth in the West Overshadows Cold in the East, Ranking Second-Warmest Nationally
item.description: The meteorological winter of December 2025 through February 2026 in the contiguous United States presented a striking regional divide. While much of the eastern US endured prolonged frigid temperature...

item.title: Nigerian Army rescues 12 kidnapped victims in Enugu, arrests two suspects
item.description: The Nigerian Army, under Operation Eastern Sanity, rescued twelve people wey dem don kidnap for Ette Community, Igbo-Eze North LGA, Enugu State. The rescue operation happen on Friday after the Army re...

item.title: Satirical Look at Canada's Ambiguous Foreign Policy on Iran
item.description: This satirical piece imagines a week in the life of former Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney, focusing on Canada's foreign policy stance regarding Iran. Initially, Carney is portrayed as strongly su...

item.title: PM directs ministers to formulate plan for petroleum conservation and public relief
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif on Saturday instructed the finance and petroleum ministers to coordinate with provincial governments and develop a comprehensive strategy for conserving petroleum product...

item.title: Homa Bay Doctors Resume Duty After Signing Return to Work Agreement with County Government
item.description: Doctors in Homa Bay county have officially ended their strike after reaching a Return to Work agreement with Governor Gladys Wanga's administration. The Kenya Medical Practitioners, Pharmacists and De...

item.title: Escalating US-Israel Conflict with Iran Drives Oil Prices Up and Adds Economic Uncertainty to a Slowing US Economy
item.description: The article analyzes the economic implications of the ongoing war in the Middle East, triggered by joint US and Israeli military strikes on Iran starting February 28, 2026. These actions have caused s...

item.title: Kashmir restrictions lifted after week-long unrest related to Iran protests
item.description: In the region of Kashmir, authorities have removed the restrictions imposed last week following protests triggered by news related to the death of Iran's Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Khamenei. These rest...

item.title: Tshwane Residents Advised to Take Precautions Ahead of Severe Thunderstorms
item.description: The City of Tshwane Emergency Services Department has issued a warning to residents as severe thunderstorms are expected on Friday, 6 March 2026. The South African Weather Service (SAWS) has declared ...

item.title: Ian Huntley, convicted of Soham murders, dies in prison after assault
item.description: Ian Huntley, the former school caretaker convicted of murdering two ten-year-old girls, Holly Wells and Jessica Chapman, in Soham in 2002, has died following an attack in prison. Huntley, aged 52, sus...

item.title: Potential Global Fertiliser Shortage from Strait of Hormuz Disruption Could Affect Food Prices
item.description: Di news explain say di current tension with Iran wey fit lead to closing di Strait of Hormuz no go affect only oil and gas, but e get strong impact on global fertiliser supply. Dis strait na key route...

item.title: Government Welcomes Sentencing of Former MP Vincent Smith for Corruption
item.description: The South African government has welcomed the sentencing of former Member of Parliament Vincent Smith, who was handed seven years' imprisonment for fraud, corruption, and related offences linked to st...

item.title: VP Sara claims President Marcos was aware of impeachment plans against her
item.description: Vice President Sara Duterte revealed that she confronted President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr. in November 2023 regarding attempts in the House of Representatives to impeach her over alleged misus...

item.title: Understanding the fascination with Generation Z: behaviour, trends and stereotypes
item.description: Generation Z, defined as those born between 1997 and 2012, has become one of the most scrutinised cohorts in modern history. Their behaviours, from binge drinking and traditional gender perspectives t...

item.title: Escalation of Israel-Iran-US Conflict in West Asia and Regional Impacts
item.description: The ongoing conflict between Israel, Iran, and the United States has intensified, now entering its second week. Following joint Israeli-US airstrikes that killed Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei an...

item.title: Creamline Cool Smashers maintain winning streak with victory over Nxled Chameleons
item.description: In a thrilling Premier Volleyball League match, the Creamline Cool Smashers managed to hold off a determined challenge from the Nxled Chameleons to continue their winning streak. The match showcased e...

item.title: Canberra Raiders kick off new NRL season against Manly at Brookvale
item.description: The Canberra Raiders are entering the new NRL season on the back of last year’s minor premiership, aiming to maintain the momentum that carried them to success. Their first challenge is against Manly ...

item.title: Kenya Faces Temporary Shortage of Rotavac Vaccine
item.description: The Ministry of Health in Kenya has issued a warning that the country is facing a temporary shortage of the Rotavac rotavirus vaccine following disruptions in global supply. Currently, only about 4,00...

item.title: Territorial Dispute Between San Juan and Lobo Halts Hiking on Mt. Daguldol, Opinion Piece Calls for Open Access and Conservation
